title: EU electrification goal | Trump’s ‘climate critic’ | Microsoft’s emissions ‘surge’ - Carbon Brief
gist: Carbon Brief's July 10, 2026 daily briefing rounds up an EU push toward a 2040 electrification target, a Trump-appointed climate skeptic taking over the US climate assessment office, China's carbon-peaking roadmap, a deadly European heatwave straining hospitals and grids, and surging AI-driven emissions at Microsoft (+27%), Google (+18%) and Amazon (+16%) as datacentre buildout collides with hyperscalers' climate pledges.

insights.claim: The European Commission plans to unveil a 2040 electrification target, expressed as a share of total EU energy consumption, in a policy plan due 17 July 2026, with the aim of embedding it into law via the post-2030 energy framework proposal later this year.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A binding EU-wide electrification target would reshape demand for heat pumps, EVs and grid infrastructure across the bloc; watch for the 17 July unveiling and the post-2030 energy framework law later in 2026.
insights.quote: the commission will unveil the target in a policy plan scheduled for 17 July, then aim to embed it into a law as part of its post-2030 energy framework proposal towards the end of the year.
insights.locator: EU plans 2040 electrification target to cut oil, gas consumption

insights.claim: EU officials estimate that faster electrification could replace around two-thirds of EU gas demand and halve oil consumption by 2040.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: If realized, this would be a major structural decline in EU fossil fuel import demand, with implications for European gas and oil suppliers and for electrification-linked equipment makers (heat pumps, EVs, grid gear).
insights.quote: could replace around two-thirds of EU gas demand and halve oil consumption by 2040
insights.locator: EU plans 2040 electrification target to cut oil, gas consumption

insights.claim: To drive electrification, the EU is considering mandating heat pumps in public buildings, tightening EV procurement targets, and phasing out fossil fuel subsidies to make electricity more cost-competitive with oil and gas.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Concrete policy levers (public-building mandates, procurement rules, subsidy removal) signal where near-term EU regulatory demand creation will land for electrification suppliers.
insights.quote: mandating the installation of heat pumps in public buildings
insights.locator: EU plans 2040 electrification target to cut oil, gas consumption

insights.claim: IEA head Fatih Birol has urged the EU to reconsider its opposition to Arctic oil and gas exploration so it can access all available Norwegian oil, a notably pro-fossil-fuel push from the world's leading energy policy body even as the EU pursues an electrification agenda.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A split between the IEA's fossil-security advocacy and the EU's electrification/decarbonization push is a tension point for European energy security policy and Arctic drilling investment.
insights.quote: urged the EU to reconsider its opposition to Arctic oil and gas exploration so it can access “every drop of oil from Norway”
insights.locator: MORE ON EU

insights.claim: Projects linked to the EU's net-zero targets will be classified as 'green by default' and exempted from the green spending requirements that otherwise govern the EU's next seven-year budget.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: An automatic green-by-default carve-out for net-zero-linked projects removes a compliance hurdle and could accelerate capital flow into EU climate infrastructure projects.
insights.quote: green by default
insights.locator: MORE ON EU

insights.claim: Sweden is leading resistance to a push by 10 EU member states to weaken the EU Emissions Trading Scheme, with a policy confrontation expected the following week.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A live fight over EU ETS strength is a near-term catalyst for European carbon price volatility; watch member-state alignment in the coming week's showdown.
insights.quote: policy showdown next week
insights.locator: MORE ON EU

insights.claim: A prospective EU ban on funding for Chinese-made solar and wind inverters risks slowing or halting renewable project rollout in poorer European countries that depend on public financing.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A China-inverter funding ban could bottleneck renewable buildout in lower-income EU member states and open opportunity for non-Chinese inverter suppliers, but at a near-term cost to project timelines.
insights.quote: could slow or even halt the rollout of solar and wind projects in poorer European countries that rely on public money
insights.locator: MORE ON EU

themes.theme: US climate policy reversal under Trump

insights.claim: The Trump administration appointed Matthew Wielicki, a self-described climate skeptic who says the science on climate change 'is not settled,' to lead the US Global Change Research Program, putting a critic of mainstream climate science in charge of the congressionally mandated National Climate Assessment released every four years.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Leadership of the National Climate Assessment by a climate skeptic signals continued institutional erosion of US federal climate science under Trump, with downstream effects on federal climate risk disclosure and policy.
insights.quote: the science is not settled on climate change
insights.locator: US: Trump taps global warming critic to reopen nation’s climate change office

insights.claim: The EPA is proposing to ease Biden-era heavy-truck tailpipe emissions rules, repealing some but not all provisions of a 2023 clean-air rule targeting nitrogen oxide pollution, without mandating any specific compliance technology such as EVs, hydrogen fuel cells, or higher fuel efficiency.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Loosened truck emissions rules reduce near-term regulatory pressure favoring electric/hydrogen heavy trucks in the US, benefiting incumbent diesel-engine and fossil-fuel-vehicle makers.
insights.quote: repeal some, but not all, provisions in a clean-air rule that the Biden administration finalised in 2023
insights.locator: US EPA proposes easing Biden heavy-truck emissions rules

insights.claim: The cost of balancing power supplies on the largest US grid has spiked to extreme levels during 2026's extreme weather events, exposing strain from overloaded transmission lines compounded by booming datacentre electricity demand.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Rising grid-balancing costs tied to AI datacentre load growth is a concrete, currently-materializing cost signal for grid operators, utilities, and any company depending on US grid capacity for new datacentre buildout.
insights.quote: exposing strains from overloaded transmission lines and booming datacentre demand
insights.locator: MORE ON US

insights.claim: China's action plan for carbon peaking during the 15th five-year plan period sets 2030 targets of 300GW of new energy storage capacity and more than 50GW of maximum regulation capacity from virtual power plants.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Explicit gigawatt-scale storage and VPP targets give investors a concrete demand forecast for Chinese battery-storage and grid-flexibility suppliers through 2030.
insights.quote: 300GW of new energy storage capacity and more than 50GW of maximum regulation capacity of virtual power plants
insights.locator: China issues action plan for carbon peaking during 15th ‘five-year plan’ period

insights.claim: China's plan calls for fully phasing out coal-fired power units that fail to meet environmental protection, energy efficiency, coal-use, safety or age requirements, alongside enhancing the power system's ability to integrate renewable energy.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A criteria-based coal retirement policy (rather than a blanket phase-out) signals selective, ongoing coal capacity attrition in China that investors should track plant-by-plant rather than assume a hard national deadline.
insights.quote: fully phasing out coal-fired power units that fail to meet environmental protection, energy efficiency, coal-use, safety or age requirements
insights.locator: China issues action plan for carbon peaking during 15th ‘five-year plan’ period

insights.claim: China aims for new energy vehicles to account for 30% of all vehicles on the road by 2030.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A 30% NEV fleet-share target by 2030 is a concrete demand anchor for Chinese EV makers, battery suppliers, and charging infrastructure.
insights.quote: China also aims to have new energy vehicles (NEVs) account for 30% of all vehicles by 2030
insights.locator: China issues action plan for carbon peaking during 15th ‘five-year plan’ period

themes.theme: AI datacentre buildout colliding with climate pledges

insights.claim: Microsoft's emissions rose 27% in its latest fiscal year to 21.1m tonnes of CO2 equivalent — the second consecutive annual increase — driven by new datacentre construction and a paused purchase of some renewable energy credits, pushing emissions further above the level recorded when it set its 2030 carbon-negative goal in 2020.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A hyperscaler's stated 2030 carbon-negative goal is now moving further out of reach as AI infrastructure investment accelerates — a credibility risk for corporate net-zero pledges across the sector.
insights.quote: Microsoft’s emissions increased 27% in its latest fiscal year, reaching 21.1m tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent
insights.locator: Microsoft emissions surge 27% as AI buildout crimps climate goals

insights.claim: Microsoft's emissions surge follows similarly worsening disclosures from Google (up 18%) and Amazon (up 16%), suggesting a sector-wide pattern in which AI infrastructure buildout is outpacing hyperscalers' climate commitments.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A consistent cross-hyperscaler pattern of rising emissions tied to AI buildout suggests this is a structural industry trend, not a one-off, relevant to ESG-linked investment screens on big tech.
insights.quote: the disclosure follows similar reports of 18% and 16% emissions increases from Google andAmazon, respectively
insights.locator: Microsoft emissions surge 27% as AI buildout crimps climate goals

insights.claim: Planned Texas datacentres could emit more greenhouse gases than many entire countries, with regulators approving new sites without public notice or environmental review.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A regulatory gap allowing unchecked datacentre emissions growth in Texas is a tail risk for future environmental-compliance costs or backlash as scrutiny of AI's carbon footprint increases.
insights.quote: planned Texas datacentres could emit more greenhouse gases than many countries
insights.locator: MORE ON TECH

insights.claim: A datacentre operator in Italy has purchased three hydropower dams to directly power its operations, illustrating a trend of AI infrastructure firms vertically integrating into power generation to secure low-carbon electricity supply.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Direct ownership of power generation assets by datacentre operators is an emerging capital-allocation pattern worth tracking as a hedge against grid-power scarcity and price volatility.
insights.quote: A datacentre operator in Italy has purchased three hydropower dams to power its operations
insights.locator: MORE ON TECH

insights.claim: Trump administration policy and surging AI datacentre electricity demand are together boosting the prospects of nuclear power in the US.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: AI-driven power demand plus favorable federal policy is a tailwind for US nuclear developers and utilities pursuing new nuclear capacity or life extensions.
insights.quote: how Trump and AI datacentres are boosting nuclear power
insights.locator: MORE ON TECH

themes.theme: Europe's heatwave strains economy and health system

insights.claim: The UK saw a record average of 80,000 people visiting A&E daily in June 2026 as temperatures hit new highs, with one hospital in Southampton reportedly brought 'very close to collapse.'
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Record heat-driven A&E demand is a direct, measurable strain on UK healthcare capacity, relevant to public-health infrastructure spending and NHS resourcing debates.
insights.quote: an average of 80,000 people visited A&E every day in June
insights.locator: UK swelters in third heatwave of the year as western Europe counts cost of hottest-ever June

insights.claim: UK retail footfall dropped 3.4% year-on-year in June amid extreme heat, and one in five school sessions in England were missed during the peak of the heatwave.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Quantified heat-driven declines in retail footfall and school attendance show extreme heat is now a measurable drag on UK consumer spending and productivity, not just a health issue.
insights.quote: total UK retail footfall dropped by 3.4% last month compared to the previous year
insights.locator: UK swelters in third heatwave of the year as western Europe counts cost of hottest-ever June

insights.claim: Britain's energy system operator issued its third heat-related warning of the summer that extreme temperatures could threaten power supplies, reflecting rising grid stress from household cooling demand.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Repeated heat-driven grid warnings in the UK point to growing peak-demand risk from rising AC/cooling adoption, a signal for UK grid capacity and flexibility investment needs.
insights.quote: warned that “extreme temperatures” could hit power supplies
insights.locator: UK swelters in third heatwave of the year as western Europe counts cost of hottest-ever June

insights.claim: Eurostar has ordered new trains rated to operate at temperatures up to 55C, preparing for the prospect of 'Saudi-style summers' in the UK and parts of Europe.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Infrastructure operators are already capital-planning for much higher peak temperatures, a concrete signal of the physical-adaptation capex cycle now underway in European transport infrastructure.
insights.quote: Eurostar has ordered trains that can operate at 55C
insights.locator: UK swelters in third heatwave of the year as western Europe counts cost of hottest-ever June

insights.claim: UK Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch says she will reject prospective Conservative parliamentary candidates who still believe in net-zero targets, framing net zero as making bills more expensive and destroying industry.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A major UK opposition party formally screening candidates on net-zero opposition signals net zero could become a binary electoral wedge issue in the UK, raising policy-continuity risk for UK climate/energy investment.
insights.quote: If someone still believes in net-zero targets that make energy more expensive and are destroying industry, they are not coming back.
insights.locator: The Conservative party is for serious people, not a retirement home for failed politicians

insights.claim: Historian Jean-Baptiste Fressoz argues that the IPCC, despite being accused of alarmism by the far right, has actually been too cautious and has underestimated the speed and impact of climate change.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A prominent argument that official climate bodies systematically understate risk supports higher-tail-risk pricing of physical climate impacts than IPCC baseline scenarios imply.
insights.quote: has, in fact, been too cautious, underestimating the speed of change and its impacts
insights.locator: MORE COMMENT

insights.claim: New research identifies the Sahel as a key hotspot for 'compound heat-drought events,' where high-temperature and low-precipitation extremes occur simultaneously.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Identification of the Sahel as a compound-hazard hotspot is relevant to agricultural, humanitarian, and climate-risk-insurance exposure assessments in the region.
insights.quote: The Sahel is a key hotspot for “compound heat-drought events” – when high-temperature and low-precipitation extremes co-occur
insights.locator: Research

insights.claim: A new study finds that significant Arctic warming preceded the peak intensity of two extreme North American cold events (2013-14 and 2017-18) by four to seven days, pointing to a possible Arctic-to-midlatitude cold-snap forecasting signal.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: A multi-day Arctic-warming lead indicator for extreme North American cold events could improve forecasting for energy demand spikes and grid-stress planning in winter.
insights.quote: “Significant Arctic warming” preceded the peak intensity of two extreme cold events in North America in 2013-14 and 2017-18 by 4-7 days
insights.locator: Research

insights.claim: Research finds that women's empowerment in sub-Saharan Africa increases the likelihood of improved household resilience to climate change, linking gender-equity outcomes to climate adaptation capacity.
insights.speaker: Carbon Brief
insights.so_what: Evidence linking women's empowerment to climate resilience supports the case for gender-focused development programs as a climate-adaptation investment, not just a social-policy goal.
insights.quote: Women's empowerment in sub-Saharan Africa increases the likelihood of improved household resilience to climate change
insights.locator: Research
